what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment ledger entry that records a board_items attachment linking the dragnet-development channel to the board item board://item-80c37615, with an added timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
Its target is a board:// URI rather than a local file
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format
It lives in the channel-memory/channel_attachments directory
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to point at an external board item
The file is a minimal, content-free ledger record
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Follows the channel-memory attachment naming pattern att-<uuid>.md
Uses a URI scheme for cross-system referencing
notes: A minimal ledger entry that attaches board item item-80c37615 to the dragnet-development channel for later lookup.
questions: What content or state does board://item-80c37615 represent?
Why was this specific board item attached to the dragnet-development channel?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records a board_items attachment to a specific board item and nothing more.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, queryable association between a channel and a specific board item so the item can be surfaced as part of channel context.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as the target value in a channel attachment record.
contribution: It contributes a machine-readable, timestamped association between the dragnet-development channel and board item item-80c37615, allowing channel memory to point outward to board items without embedding their content.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment ledger file that records a board_items link from the dragnet-development channel to board item item-80c37615 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persistently associate a board item with a channel so the attachment can be discovered in channel memory queries.
